sourceMovie.title: Meet the Parents
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Meet the Parents for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jay Roach keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Proposal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Meet the Parents clicked for you, The Proposal (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Meet the Parents?
faq.answer: Try The Proposal, Just Go with It, Meet the Fockers—each shares DNA with Meet the Parents while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Meet the Parents?
faq.answer: Meet the Parents (2000) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Meet the Parents part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Meet the Parents Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
